macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Middle East war pushes up global energy and commodity prices
items.summary: Recent market and bank commentary says the conflict in Iran has driven oil and natural gas prices sharply higher, lifting global commodity costs. That is feeding through into inflation expectations in both the euro area and the US.

items.title: Euro area inflation accelerates to 3.0% as energy costs jump
items.summary: KBC Bank said euro area inflation rose from 2.6% to 3.0%, mainly because of another surge in energy prices. Core inflation eased slightly, but rising import and producer prices suggest more inflation pressure ahead.

items.title: US inflation rises to 3.8% amid higher energy and food prices
items.summary: KBC Bank reported that US inflation increased from 3.3% to 3.8%, with energy the biggest driver and food prices also rising sharply. Core inflation ticked up as well, reinforcing concerns about sticky price pressures.

items.title: RBC raises US Q2 growth outlook as economy shows resilience
items.summary: RBC said US Q1 growth came in better than expected and that it is lifting its Q2 GDP forecast to 2.4%. The bank described the economy as split between solid growth and persistent inflation risks, with the labor market still stable.

items.title: US markets brace for sticky inflation and delayed Fed easing
items.summary: RBC said inflation remains near 3% and is being reinforced by the energy shock, while services inflation stays firm. The bank expects the Federal Reserve to stay on the sidelines through the rest of 2026 and 2027.

items.title: Key US data calendar includes GDP, Core PCE, and consumer confidence releases
items.summary: Trading Economics' calendar shows a cluster of important US macro releases, including GDP growth, the Core PCE price index, housing data, and consumer confidence. These reports are the main near-term catalysts for rates and equities.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin slips below $77K as crypto market turns mixed
items.summary: Bitcoin traded around $76,749 to $77,390 over the past day, with reports pointing to ETF outflows, macro headwinds, and institutional selling pressure. Ethereum also moved lower while several altcoins outperformed, including NEAR, ONDO, and WLD.

items.title: NEAR, ONDO, and WLD lead altcoin gains amid broader market weakness
items.summary: NEAR surged about 28.6% on AI-related narrative and ecosystem growth, while ONDO gained roughly 15.4% and WLD rose 12.6% on tokenization and adoption-related optimism. Most major cryptocurrencies remained range-bound despite the selective strength in these names.

items.title: Privacy and post-quantum tokens rally on quantum-computing concerns
items.summary: Privacy-focused assets and post-quantum tokens jumped as investors worried about long-term cryptographic risks from quantum computing. QRL led the move with a sharp gain, while Zcash also rose to a year-to-date high.

items.title: U.S. Treasury sanctions crypto network tied to Sinaloa Cartel
items.summary: The Treasury announced sanctions against a cash-to-crypto network allegedly used by drug traffickers connected to the Sinaloa Cartel. The action adds to recent U.S. enforcement pressure on illicit crypto payment channels.

items.title: Missouri sues CoinFlip over alleged crypto ATM scams and high fees
items.summary: Missouri filed a lawsuit accusing CoinFlip of crypto ATM scams and massive transaction fees. The case highlights growing state-level scrutiny of consumer protections in crypto kiosks.

items.title: Blockchain.com files for U.S. IPO
items.summary: Blockchain.com has reportedly filed for a U.S. initial public offering, signaling renewed appetite among crypto firms to access public markets. The move follows a broader wave of industry expansion and fundraising activity.

items.title: Securitize plans SPAC merger to go public and scale tokenization
items.summary: Tokenization platform Securitize is planning a SPAC merger as it looks to expand its market reach and infrastructure. The development underscores continued institutional interest in real-world asset tokenization.

items.title: Gemini expands stablecoin ecosystem with USAT, XAUT, and USDT0
items.summary: Gemini added several stablecoin-related assets as it broadens its product lineup. The move reflects ongoing competition among exchanges to build deeper stablecoin liquidity and utility.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: GTA Online weekly update highlights double payouts and new challenge rewards
items.summary: A gaming update video highlights this week’s GTA Online bonuses, including double money and RP on RC Bandito time trials and Hot Ring Races. It also notes a weekly challenge reward tied to LS Car Series wins.

items.title: IGN discusses a new look at Yoshi and the Mysterious Book
items.summary: IGN’s Nintendo Voice Chat appears to cover fresh information or impressions about Yoshi and the Mysterious Book, suggesting continued attention around the upcoming game. The clip is framed as a gaming-news discussion rather than a formal release announcement.

items.title: Retro Shooter Fusion showcased as a large-scale arcade setup
items.summary: A new video presents the Retro Shooter Fusion, described as a 24TB retro arcade experience built by combining hardware and content for a high-end arcade-style setup. The clip is more of a product showcase than a formal industry announcement, but it may interest retro gaming enthusiasts.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Quantum computing stocks surge after U.S. government grants with equity stakes
items.summary: Shares of major quantum computing firms jumped after the U.S. government announced $2 billion in grants to nine companies in the sector. The move gave investors fresh confidence in the industry and lifted names like D-Wave, Rigetti, and IBM.

items.title: Google unveils new Search changes and expands AI-adjacent features at I/O
items.summary: Google's latest I/O announcements included major changes to Search and several product updates across its ecosystem. The company also highlighted new consumer hardware and software features aimed at broadening its platform reach.
items.source: Tech news coverage via YouTube roundup

items.title: Meta quietly launches standalone Forum app for Facebook Groups
items.summary: Meta released a new standalone app called Forum that surfaces Facebook Groups conversations in a dedicated feed. The app also includes an Ask feature and is now available on iOS.

items.title: Google adds new features to Fitbit and YouTube
items.summary: Google made additional product changes across its ecosystem, including a major Fitbit identity update and a new Ask YouTube search feature. These updates continue Google's push to streamline its consumer products.

items.title: Samsung previews new XR smart glasses with partners
items.summary: Samsung previewed its Galaxy XR smart glasses along with versions developed with Warby Parker and Gentle Monster. The announcement signals another push by a major hardware maker into extended reality devices.

items.title: Apple releases Safari Technology Preview 244
items.summary: Apple issued Safari Technology Preview 244 with bug fixes and performance improvements. The release is aimed at developers and early testers of upcoming browser changes.

items.title: Oura files confidentially for U.S. IPO
items.summary: Smart ring maker Oura has confidentially filed for an initial public offering in the United States. The filing suggests growing investor interest in wearable hardware companies.

items.title: Serverless AI infrastructure startup Modal Labs raises $355 million
items.summary: Modal Labs secured a $355 million funding round to expand its serverless computing platform. The deal underscores continued investor appetite for cloud infrastructure startups.
